name: Don Costa
description: American recording artist, conductor, record producer, music arranger, jazz guitarist (1925–1983)
abstract: Dominick P. "Don" Costa (June 10, 1925 – January 19, 1983) was an American conductor and record producer.

about_quotes.speaker: Carmen Caramanica
about_quotes.evidence: this particular one, was called “The Soul of Nigger Charlie,” ... Well this was arranged by a guy named Don Costa ... and I was brought in as a guitar soloist ... we go through and we play the thing
about_quotes.doc_title: Carmen Caramanica interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, July 27, 2011

about_quotes.speaker: Ralph Humphrey
about_quotes.evidence: he called me up and said do you want to come down and audition for the band... I went ahead and went down for the audition... playing with the band
about_quotes.doc_title: Ralph Humphrey interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: John Pizzarelli, Jr.
about_quotes.evidence: I found good records. You know the Sinatra records are great because, especially that “Sinatra and Strings” because the bass notes are there and Costa’s, Don Costa’s chords to those songs, those ten songs, are so great.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Pizzarelli, Jr. interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Rudy Van Gelder
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Don Costa buying stand lights for the interviewer, not the interviewee. The interviewee does not mention any personal interaction or relationship with Don Costa.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rudy Van Gelder — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Barbutti
about_quotes.evidence: But I’m not in the same class as Don Costa or those guys who would just be on an airplane and take a paper and write a chart for Sinatra
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Barbutti interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 8, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Sauter
about_quotes.evidence: Don Costa got the idea we should try something for United Artists, which we hadn't done, but we did it.
